query: Lee Jae-myung approval rating Gallup Korea Realmeter late June early July 2026, is approval still falling, what is driving the decline, any dead cross
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on Lee Jae-myung's approval ratings for late June/early July 2026:

**Is approval still falling?** The multi-week decline **bottomed out in mid/late June and modestly reversed** in early July — but with an important pollster split:

- **Gallup Korea:** Hit a post-inauguration low of **51%** (June 26), then rebounded to **54%** (July 3, vs 36% disapproval). Never reached a dead cross.
- **Realmeter:** Registered its **first-ever dead cross** on June 22 (46.7% approval vs 49.7% disapproval), and the dead cross **persists for a 3rd consecutive week** as of July 6 (47.0% vs 49.2%), despite a slight uptick.

**Dead cross:** YES in Realmeter (3 straight weeks, first of Lee's presidency); NO in Gallup.

**What's driving the decline:** National Election Commission (NEC) irregularities scandal and accountability debate, economy/livelihood concerns, housing policy, and ruling-bloc internal conflict.

**What drove the July rebound:** The "three mega-projects" announcements (Samsung/SK hynix investments in semiconductors, AI, data centers under "balanced regional development"), plus economy handling and diplomacy — though Realmeter noted stock market declines and high exchange rates capped the recovery.

The Gallup/Realmeter divergence reflects the recurring methodological gap (Realmeter's automated telephone polling consistently shows lower presidential approval than Gallup's live-interviewer method).

sources.title: Lee's approval rating sinks to lowest point since taking office: Gallup poll
sources.snippet: Approval dropped to 51%, lowest since taking office; disapproval 41%, first time exceeding 40% in Gallup. Factors: economy, housing, NEC irregularities.

sources.title: Lee's approval rating rebounds to 54% after dipping to lowest level - Gallup poll
sources.snippet: Approval rose 3pp to 54%; disapproval 36%. Democratic Party 41%, PPP 26%. Economy (24%) and diplomacy (14%) top positive reasons; three mega-projects cited.

sources.title: 이 대통령 국정지지율 47%…7주 만에 반등에도 '데드크로스' 지속 [리얼미터]
sources.snippet: Realmeter July 6: approval 47.0%, disapproval 49.2%, dead cross 3rd straight week. DP 43.0%, PPP 40.3%. Stock decline and high FX limited gains.

sources.title: 李대통령 지지율 50% 깨졌다… 취임 후 첫 '데드크로스' [리얼미터]
sources.snippet: Realmeter June 22: approval 46.7%, disapproval 49.7% — first dead cross since inauguration, 5 straight weeks of decline. Cites NEC mismanagement accountability and ruling-bloc turmoil.

sources.title: [리얼미터] 이재명 대통령 지지율 '데드크로스'…민주당은 반등 '디커플링'
sources.snippet: Realmeter June week 3: approval 46.7%, disapproval 49.7%; president's rating falls while Democratic Party recovers ('decoupling').

sources.title: Lee's approval rating dips to 64 pct over 'excessive' welfare, spending concerns
sources.snippet: Earlier (May 1) Gallup: approval slipped 3pp to 64%, welfare/spending concerns cited — context for the longer decline trajectory.

key_facts: Gallup Korea (published June 26, 2026): Lee's approval fell to 51% (−6pp), disapproval rose to 41% (+6pp) — lowest since inauguration; disapproval topped 40% for first time in Gallup. No dead cross.
Gallup Korea (published July 3, 2026): approval rebounded to 54% (+3pp), disapproval fell to 36% (−5pp). Democratic Party 41%, People Power Party 26%.
Realmeter (published June 22, 2026): approval 46.7% (−4.8pp), disapproval 49.7% (+5.5pp) — FIRST dead cross of Lee's presidency; 5th consecutive week of decline.
Realmeter (published July 6, 2026): approval 47.0% (+0.5pp), disapproval 49.2% (−0.3pp) — dead cross persists for 3rd consecutive week despite slight uptick.
Realmeter July 6 party support: Democratic Party 43.0% (+2.0pp), People Power Party 40.3% (−1.7pp); gap narrowed to 2.7pp (within margin of error).
Decline drivers: National Election Commission (NEC) irregularities scandal and accountability debate, economy/livelihoods, housing/real estate policy, ruling-party internal conflict.

July rebound drivers: 'three mega-projects' announcements (Samsung Electronics and SK hynix investments in semiconductors, AI, data centers / balanced regional development); economy handling and diplomacy cited as top positive reasons.
Realmeter noted stock market decline and high won/dollar exchange rates constrained the July recovery.
Pollster divergence: Realmeter (automated telephone, larger sample) shows dead cross; Gallup (live interviewer) shows approval still leading — a recurring methodological pattern.

data_points.metric: Realmeter consecutive weeks of dead cross (as of July 6)
